Music CRS Recall Gap Explorer

Interactive diagnosis of the v0plus_compiler_all_retrievers_devset devset run. The framing follows the requested rule: if the ground-truth item is not in branch union@20 or union@100, that is a gap, even if it appears deeper in union@200 or union@1000.

Generated 2026-06-06 12:34:49 8,000 devset turns branch_trace_topk=1000 Current baseline fusion: RRF k=60
Overview Report map Work queue Bad examples State plan Gap explorer Sources

Executive Summary

  • The largest opportunity is not a new retriever by itself. Final Hit@20 is 27.4%, but branch union@100 is 66.2%; the target design is a state-aware candidate scorer over the retrieved pool, not another tweak to the current equal-rank fusion baseline.
  • Union@20 and union@100 expose two different problems. Union@20 misses are often ranker/final-policy losses; union@100 misses are more often weak retrieval or latent target failures where the GT entity is absent from state.
  • State is useful, but some state is being used too bluntly. Same-artist demotion under diversify_artists and literal release-range handling bury many plausible GTs.
  • Retriever improvements should target latent targets. When no GT track or artist appears in state, union@100 collapses. Improve tag-popularity, similar-artist, lyric, visual, and hidden-target routes before adding broad embedding branches.
  • The scoring lens changed the priority queue. Codabench weights nDCG@20 at 0.50, LLM-Judge at 0.30, LexicalDiversity at 0.10, and CatalogDiversity at 0.10. Retrieval work still matters most, but lm_type=dummy leaves a large response lane untouched.

Ceiling Ladder

Read this as an experiment map: trained ranking attacks the union@20/100 ceiling; better retrievers attack the gap between union@100 and union@1000.

Report map

Where To Go For Each Question

Use this as the only cross-report map. The sticky nav above moves within this report; the cards below open deeper reports or review artifacts.

Read first Main recall report Headline metrics, work queue, global gap explorer, examples, and sources. Stay on this page Best for state bugs State case studies Truncated conversation, returned tracks, extracted state, why wrong, and ideal state. state_focus/#examples Best for extractor work Schema audit + replay packs Field decisions, ideal state targets, field economy, and 10-turn small test packs. state_focus/#schema-audit Best for scorer work Ranker / retriever misses Concrete near misses, union gaps, and ranker/retriever decision evidence. ranker_decision/#examples-section

Evidence funnel

Find vs Ship, Then What To Work On

27.4%
Ships today
2,189 final top-20 hits
47.7%
Union@20
Strict first boundary: already candidate-visible
58.5%
Union@50
Shallow rerank/retrieval-profile workbench
66.2%
Union@100
Honest first-stage ranker ceiling for this pool
90.5%
Union@1000
Deep oracle reference, not the next target
How to read this: union@20 is still the strict gap line; union@100 is the honest first-stage ranker ceiling for the current pool; union@1000 is useful as a retrieval-capability reference, not as the immediate target. Anything below union@100 needs state/routing/retriever work, not only a better final scorer.

Bugs And Missing Work To Incorporate

This queue is intentionally colored by action type: red = fix or validate now, amber = high-value follow-up, teal = avoid over-investing first. It is the compact checklist to carry into the next extraction/ranker cycle.

P0 bug

Wire routing tags into retrieval profiles

Evidence: 0 routing-tag firings, but current routing weights are empty; hidden_target_search alone fires 0.

Incorporate: Set routing_boost/profile weights and replay by exact_entity_probe, feature_articulation, hidden_target_search, lyric, and visual slices.

P0 state

Replace roleless +1 artist carryover with keep-strength roles

Evidence: Stale positive entities and novelty-prior-anchor conflicts explain why continuation wins while discovery collapses.

Incorporate: Extract seed, satisfied, contrast, history, rejected, source_turn, recency, and use_as_retrieval_seed; validate on the 110-turn pack before a full rerun.

P0 scorer

Train candidate scorer on practical union pools

Evidence: final@20 27.4% vs union@100 66.2%; new-artist union@20 remains weak, so ranker and routing must move together.

Incorporate: Use union@100/200 as the workbench; include branch ranks, artist role, album/artist recency, popularity, tag overlap, and candidate-varying goal/profile affinity.

P0 score

Add response generation evaluation

Evidence: Codabench weights response quality at 0.30 and lexical diversity at 0.10; this run uses lm_type=dummy.

Incorporate: Run a real generator over dev predictions, measure Distinct-2, and inspect Gemini-judge style/grounding before prompt tuning.

P1 policy

Turn same-artist/album demotion into learned features

Evidence: Post-fusion loss is dominated by same_artist_as_played (659) and diversify_artists (626).

Incorporate: Keep diversity as a feature, but add trusted-survivor slots and album/artist-recency features for continuation turns.

P1 guardrail

Separate style-era from hard release-date constraints and assert rejections

Evidence: 634 turns exclude the GT year; strict rejection leaks are a verified lower bound with name-match cases as an upper audit bound.

Incorporate: Soft-penalize era-style mismatches, keep hard ranges only for literal date asks, and add final strict-ID rejection assertions.

Do not start

Do not chase raw session constants or union@1000 first

Evidence: Raw category/specificity/demographics are candidate-constant in a within-turn ranker; union@1000 is a huge-pool oracle.

Incorporate: Use metadata as routing context or candidate-varying affinity; use union@20 as the gap line and union@100/200 as the first ranker workbench.

Bad examples

Failures To Replay, With Why Wrong And What To Change

Nothing was removed: the examples were present in the data, but too buried in tabs and expandable sections. This section surfaces the replay set from the global report and links to the deeper state/ranker example pages.

State failure examples State schema audit Full 110-turn replay JSON Ranker / retriever misses Gap explorer

State role bugs visible on this page

These are the hand-auditable extraction failures: the state kept an old or contrast entity as a positive retrieval anchor. The fix is not just “more state”; it is role-typed, recency-aware state.

State bug

e0631f94 t4

Ask: "Dorothy is awesome... more ARTISTS with powerful female vocalists"

Wrong anchor: Dorothy, Gun In My Hand

Why wrong: The user already heard Dorothy and loved it. They’re asking for OTHER artists with the same vibe. But our system treats Dorothy as a search target (+1 anchor), so it keeps returning Dorothy-like tracks instead of bran...

Change: Dorothy should be marked “satisfied” (done, don’t search for it). The search should use the style tags (female vocalist, blues-rock) to find NEW artists with a similar sound.

State bug

b4550ccd t6

Ask: "Funky Child is awesome... what else can you hit me with that kind [of vibe]"

Wrong anchor: 11 artists: Eminem, Method Man, CZARFACE, Lupe Fiasco, Freddie Gibbs...

Why wrong: The user only mentioned Funky Child just now, but our system drags in 11 artists from older messages (Eminem, Method Man, etc.) as active search targets. These old names flood the results, burying the fresh recommenda...

Change: Only Funky Child is relevant (and it's already heard — mark it 'satisfied'). All 11 old artists should be dropped from active search. Use classic/hip-hop style tags to find something new.

State bug

58855168 t8

Ask: "...keen to discover something NEW now, a DIFFERENT artist entirely" than Enya

Wrong anchor: Enya

Why wrong: The user literally says they want something NEW and DIFFERENT — not Enya. But our system treats Enya as a positive search target (+1 anchor), so it finds more Enya-like music. The user is saying 'I'm done with this, s...

Change: Enya should be marked 'contrast' — meaning actively steer AWAY from Enya's sound. Use ambient/atmospheric style tags but search for genuinely different artists.

State bug

39d9709b t3

Ask: "...another band, completely DIFFERENT [from Tenacious D]. Heavy industrial dark"

Wrong anchor: Tenacious D, Master Exploder

Why wrong: The user says 'completely DIFFERENT from Tenacious D' and asks for heavy industrial music. But our system uses Tenacious D (a comedy rock band) as a positive search target, pulling results toward comedy rock — the exa...

Change: Tenacious D should be marked 'contrast' — meaning steer AWAY from it. The search should be driven by the style the user asked for: industrial, heavy, dark.

State bug

f8d468c3 t8

Ask: "I like Snow Patrol... just send more alternative rock, ANYTHING"

Wrong anchor: Nothing But Thieves, MISSIO

Why wrong: The user says 'I like Snow Patrol… just send more alternative rock, ANYTHING.' They want a wide net. But our system anchors on Nothing But Thieves and MISSIO — two bands from earlier in the conversation that the user...

Change: Drop Nothing But Thieves and MISSIO (they're history, not current). Snow Patrol is 'satisfied' (already discussed). Cast a wide net with broad alternative-rock style tags — the user literally said 'anything.'

One replay turn per failure class

The state experiment pack contains 10 turns per failure class plus positive controls. This visible table shows one representative row for each class so the report can be skimmed before running small API-call experiments.

SampleFailure classCurrent user askGTBaseline ranksWhy wrongWhat should change
0b9d547f-e748-464a-90e2-2199149f915c::t6P0_roleless_stale_entity_failureYes! "Can You Feel the Force" is awesome, such a great track. That's exactly the kind of energy I'm looking for. What are some other high-energy, classic disco or funk tracks from that late 70s to early 80s period?Give It To Me Baby by Rick Jamesfinal=158; fused=218; branch=101 (dense.qwen_8b.attributes.attributes_qwen3_embedding_8b)The user asks for novelty or a different direction, but the state still keeps The Real Thing as positive anchors. That sends retrievers toward already-satisfied artists instead of the new target space.Add entity roles such as seed, satisfied, contrast, history, and rejected. For novelty/diversify turns, demote satisfied/history anchors as retrieval seeds and upweight tag, metadata, popularity, and CF profiles.
c7a965c3-cd7f-46f6-b166-9dce9a800e0a::t3P0_novelty_prior_anchor_failureYes! That's exactly the track! 'Toxic' by Britney Spears always makes me feel that way. It's so iconic and brings back all those confident, dancing vibes. You nailed it! Thanks! Can you recommend something else with a similar super strong, driving beat? May...S&M by Rihannafinal=163; fused=181; branch=101 (dense.qwen_8b.attributes.attributes_qwen3_embedding_8b)The user asks for novelty or a different direction, but the state still keeps Britney Spears as positive anchors. That sends retrievers toward already-satisfied artists instead of the new target space.Add entity roles such as seed, satisfied, contrast, history, and rejected. For novelty/diversify turns, demote satisfied/history anchors as retrieval seeds and upweight tag, metadata, popularity, and CF profiles.
a930da0d-07f1-46c6-909d-e4fd95ae1148::t6P0_new_artist_union20_gap_failureAnother fantastic Michael Jackson track! 'Will You Be There' is definitely a powerful and energetic song that everybody knows. These are exactly the kind of widely loved, feel-good hits I enjoy. What other well-known songs do you have that are popular and h...Without You by Christina Aguilerafinal=413; fused=423; branch=101 (centroid.anchor_tracks.cf_bpr)The target is not in a strong candidate pool (best branch 101 in centroid.anchor_tracks.cf_bpr; final 413). This is mostly a retriever/state-to-retriever coverage gap, not just a final-ranker miss.Improve turn-type routing and candidate generation: use listener_goal/current state, role-aware entities, tags, popularity, culture/CF affinity, and novelty profiles before spending effort only on final reranking.
d5fcb591-3744-4ebb-9d1a-5c57c314b7d0::t5P1_temporal_constraint_failureYes! "He's the Greatest Dancer" is such a jam! That's exactly the kind of funky, soulful sound from the late 70s I'm looking for. What else do you have from that golden era of R&B?Love Train by The O'Jaysfinal=160; fused=369; branch=106 (centroid.anchor_tracks.audio_laion_clap)The extracted release range (1975, 1984) excludes the target release year 1972. If this range is treated as a hard constraint or strong demotion, the correct item is pushed away before ranking.Split hard date constraints from stylistic era cues. Era-like wording should become a soft compatibility feature; only explicit date-bound language should hard-filter or heavily penalize candidates.
4d0afb6b-3705-493f-ab16-ca75ea311e1a::t7P1_rejection_guardrail_failureThese Blade Runner 2049 tracks are fantastic, but I've heard quite a few from this album now. I'm really looking to discover *new* artists or different film scores that have that same dark, futuristic, and melancholic electronic instrumental vibe. Please, n...Pilot by Benjamin Wallfisch, Hans Zimmerfinal=-; fused=-; branch=1 (dense.qwen_8b.metadata.metadata_qwen3_embedding_8b)The state contains an explicit rejection, but final top-20 still includes Tears In Rain by Vangelis. That is a rejected name leak, independent of whether the ground-truth target was retrieved.Add a deterministic post-final rejection filter/assertion over rejected track IDs, artist IDs, and normalized multi-artist names. Treat broad name matches as an audit sample, but make strict ID leakage impossible.
37097db6-54b8-491b-8512-1df70648548b::t2P0_named_artist_ranker_failureSeigfried" is a good pick, Frank Ocean always hits the spot. It's got that introspective vibe. Do you have anything with a more melancholic or dreamy feel, less upbeat, still for that late-night mood?White Ferrari by Frank Oceanfinal=70; fused=16; branch=1 (centroid.anchor_tracks.image_siglip2)The current user names the target artist, but White Ferrari by Frank Ocean misses final top-20 (final 70; best branch 1 in centroid.anchor_tracks.image_siglip2). The entity is understood, but track selection or final ranking fails.Protect named-artist candidate generation, then rank tracks with artist_recency, album affinity, exactness, branch-rank, and popularity features instead of relying on equal-weight RRF alone.
84803908-48e7-41b7-9269-a465a44f4c10::t2P0_same_album_ranker_failureYes, that's exactly the kind of strong, bold hip-hop I wanted! Are there other tracks with that intense, anthemic quality?Runaway by Pusha T, Kanye Westfinal=29; fused=10; branch=1 (centroid.anchor_tracks.image_siglip2)The user asks for novelty or a different direction, but the state still keeps Kanye West as positive anchors. That sends retrievers toward already-satisfied artists instead of the new target space.Add entity roles such as seed, satisfied, contrast, history, and rejected. For novelty/diversify turns, demote satisfied/history anchors as retrieval seeds and upweight tag, metadata, popularity, and CF profiles.
ad5348a7-d3bc-4882-bfca-54aa655eac96::t5P1_positive_tag_retrieval_gap_failureDope! "REDMERCEDES" is definitely on point with that vibrant energy, both in the song and the cover. These are exactly the kind of tracks I was looking for, where the artwork really brings out the music's vibe. What else have you got that fits this awesome...Glitter by Tyler, The Creatorfinal=137; fused=216; branch=101 (centroid.user.cf_bpr)The target is not in a strong candidate pool (best branch 101 in centroid.user.cf_bpr; final 137). This is mostly a retriever/state-to-retriever coverage gap, not just a final-ranker miss.Improve turn-type routing and candidate generation: use listener_goal/current state, role-aware entities, tags, popularity, culture/CF affinity, and novelty profiles before spending effort only on final reranking.
13066d2c-2d5e-4162-b3dc-354ecef3aff5::t3P0_good_state_ranker_near_miss_failurePerfect! This rock evolution journey is exactly what I was hoping for, spanning from classic roots to modern alternative! What's next on this journey? Maybe something from the late 90s or early 2000s, still keeping that alternative vibe?Yellow by Coldplayfinal=32; fused=85; branch=1 (lookup.era_popularity)The target is already reachable in a branch top-20, but final ranking drops it (best branch 1 in lookup.era_popularity; final 32). This is a fusion/ranker decision error.Replace or calibrate RRF with a state-aware scorer that sees branch ranks plus candidate state features such as role, same-album, new-artist, rejection, year distance, and tag overlap.

Positive controls to protect

These should stay good after extractor changes; use them as regression checks so a role/state rewrite does not break exact-entity and clean final-hit behavior.

Positive samplePackGTKeep this working
0681d55b-98a0-4773-a9df-075a8050d805::t1POS_exact_entity_success_controlNumb by Linkin ParkTrack union@20 and union@100 for this example after each retriever/routing change; a ranker cannot fix it until the target enters the practical union pool.
8bee6f03-8cae-44ae-9325-455dc1138549::t1POS_exact_entity_success_controlAfrica by TOTO, TotoTrack union@20 and union@100 for this example after each retriever/routing change; a ranker cannot fix it until the target enters the practical union pool.
d62387d0-3743-4ddc-bc92-8204c951ccee::t1POS_exact_entity_success_controlIn the End by Linkin ParkTrack union@20 and union@100 for this example after each retriever/routing change; a ranker cannot fix it until the target enters the practical union pool.
bd2aa024-68e7-43c2-aa87-afce9b4d7cf1::t2POS_exact_entity_success_controlShut Up and Dance by WALK THE MOONTrack union@20 and union@100 for this example after each retriever/routing change; a ranker cannot fix it until the target enters the practical union pool.

Ranker and retrieval misses

These examples separate final scorer losses from candidate-generation failures. The current fusion method is only baseline instrumentation here; the ideal target is state-aware retrieval profiles plus a trained or calibrated candidate scorer.

Gap bucketSampleGTRanksState / routeWhat should change
union20_fusion_loss0979c6fc-c382-4c14-be3e-2a4711fcc532::t1Hard Times by Cro-Magsfinal=134; fused=60; branch=6 (dense.qwen_8b.metadata.metadata_qwen3_embedding_8b)entity=no_gt_track_or_artist_in_state; policy=balanced; routing=feature_articulationGT is already in a branch top-20, but the fused/final path does not ship it. Use branch-rank features, state roles, and learned scorer/finalization guardrails.
union20_fusion_loss1f1947c0-1c27-4520-9577-66af51c463f3::t2Electronic World Transmission - Reconstructed by [:SITD:] by Rotersandfinal=35; fused=45; branch=8 (bm25)entity=no_gt_track_or_artist_in_state; policy=diversify_artists; routing=feature_articulation, image_or_visual_searchGT is already in a branch top-20, but the fused/final path does not ship it. Use branch-rank features, state roles, and learned scorer/finalization guardrails.
union20_fusion_loss1f1947c0-1c27-4520-9577-66af51c463f3::t5Ignite by Dioramafinal=35; fused=50; branch=4 (centroid.anchor_tracks.audio_laion_clap)entity=no_gt_track_or_artist_in_state; policy=diversify_artists; routing=feature_articulationGT is already in a branch top-20, but the fused/final path does not ship it. Use branch-rank features, state roles, and learned scorer/finalization guardrails.

Use retrieval-profile routing plus a trained scorer over union@100/200.

Use retrieval-profile routing plus a trained scorer over union@100/200.

State work

What To Change, What To Leave Alone

Open case studies Schema audit Replay JSON

State extraction is expensive, so this section is organized as a decision queue. Read the open summary first, then expand only the tables needed for implementation or review.

P0Split roleless entities and add target artist mode.
P0Wire state routing into retriever profiles before adding retrievers.
P1Use guardrails for temporal constraints and rejections.
Do not startDo not add raw demographics, raw tags, or duplicate mode fields first.
Decision brief: task modes and state scorecard

This restores the high-level diagnosis into the global report. Exact named tracks are mostly solved; continuation is a ranker/state-use lane; mid-conversation new-artist turns are the main candidate-generation lane.

ModeTurnsShareFinal@20Union@20Union@100Rank loss@20Candidate gap@20Read
Exact named track921.1%100.0%100.0%100.0%00This should be nearly solved; misses here are precision/state-routing bugs.
Continuation: same artist322340.3%46.3%83.3%93.6%1209537Mostly a state-use/ranking problem: the relevant artist is known, but the right track is not chosen.
New artist mid-conversation377747.2%10.0%19.4%44.9%4263046The main state/retriever problem: the system must convert state constraints into new-artist candidates.
Cold open100012.5%32.0%39.6%58.6%97604No session state exists yet, so popularity/canonicality and profile priors matter more than state repair.
State areaCurrent evidenceAudit signalDecision
Positive tag extraction7,720 turns have positive_tags; 76.9% overlap at least one GT catalog tag.positive_tags overlap a GT tag on 78% of valid turns. Extraction vocab is fine.Do not make tag extraction the first major state rewrite. Use tags better in ranker/router features.
Entity role and recency3,807 turns have positive artist/track state not present in the current user turn; 3,157 novelty turns keep prior anchors.positive artist targets climb to ~2.3 by turn 3+ vs ~1 actually referenced forward. Carries old artists forward.P0. Add seed/satisfied/contrast/history/rejected roles and decay history before anchors/discography.
Release-year state2,164 turns set a release range; 634 exclude the GT release year.of turns with a year_range set, GT falls OUTSIDE it 29% of the time (580 miss@20). Soft-penalizes valid answers.P1. Split stylistic era from hard release-date bounds; run no-year and soft-year ablations.
P0 routing config gapTrace routing tags fire 9,094 times (feature_articulation=6,748, exact_entity_probe=1,605, hidden_target_search=391), but config routing_boost is empty.Routing tags are extracted, but routing is ineffective when the boost map is empty.P0. Treat this as a config/consumption bug: wire tags into retriever profile weights before adding new retrievers.
Rejection enforcement995 turns have rejection state; 97 strict-ID leak turns plus 62 additional name-only audit turns produce 159 broad final top-20 leak flags.769 of 14,780 returned slots were a rejected artist/track despite "hard drop". Needs verification (multi-artist) but "no Bon Jovi → Bon Jovi" happens.P1. Add deterministic post-final assertions and multi-artist rejection tests.
Measured levers and what not to do first

This is the practical work list. State extraction is expensive, so measured or cheap-routing work should come before broad extractor or retriever rewrites.

LeverStatusMeasured resultDecisionSource
Album-affinity / album completionmeasured counterfactual479 of 964 same-album misses are rescuable from fused rank 21-100; 326 sit at 101-1000 and 159 are absent. Reported ceiling: about +6pp Hit@20.Keep as a P0 ranker feature: same_album_recent plus artist_recency, with continuation and new-artist guardrails.album counterfactual audit
Current trace album fused-rank anatomytrace recountPrimary-album misses=978; fused<=20/final-missed=454, fused 21-100=301, fused 101-1000=90, absent=133; bucket sum=978.Read fused<=20 as post-fusion/finalization loss, not album-retrieval absence.current trace rows
baseline-fused cross-encoder rerankermeasured adjacent-pool bakeoffNDCG@20 0.1415 -> 0.1594 (12.7% relative); Hit@20 0.3098 -> 0.3325. Scope: Adjacent full-devset reranker bakeoff on v0plus_compiler_bm25_image_audio_cfbpr_metadata_devset, not the all-retrievers trace in this report.Use rank fusion or a trained scorer over branch features; do not replace current baseline fusion with raw cross-encoder score.cross_encoder_rerank_bakeoff.md
Candidate-level is_new_artist featuremeasured small positive+1% reranker on noveltyUse as a ranker feature, but still fix candidate generation because new-artist GTs are often outside union@20.feature experiment audit
Raw session/category/demographic features in a within-turn rankermeasured no lift0 lift — candidate-constant within a turn.Do not feed raw constants directly; derive candidate-varying culture/user-CF/popularity affinity or use them for routing/slicing.feature-ablation audit
Rarity-weighted / IDF tagsmeasured negativeWORSE than raw; tags cap ~0.19 hit@50.Do not prioritize IDF-tag weighting; tags are useful, but role/routing and candidate-varying behavioral features are stronger.feature-ablation audit
Do not start withDecisionEvidence
Use union@1000 as the ranker targetDo not headline it.Union@1000 is 90.5%, but it is an oracle over very large branch pools. Use union@20 as the gap line and union@100/200 as ranker workbench.
Feed raw category/specificity/demographics directly into a within-turn rankerUse them as slices or derive candidate-varying features.Category, specificity, and demographics are session constants within a candidate list. They can condition retrieval/ranker behavior but cannot distinguish candidate A vs B unless transformed.
Overhaul tag extraction firstNot first.Positive tags overlap a GT tag on 76.9% of tagged turns; entity role/recency has a broader and clearer failure signature.
Tune the current baseline fusion aloneInsufficient.New-artist union@20 is only 19.4%. baseline-fusion replacement helps near misses, not absent candidates.
Replace the current baseline fusion with raw cross-encoder scoreDo not use replace mode.The adjacent reranker bakeoff found replace-mode reranking structurally harmful; rank-fuse current baseline fusion and reranker scores or train a scorer over branch/state features.
IDF-weight positive tagsNot first.WORSE than raw; tags cap ~0.19 hit@50.
Continuation vs new-artist deep dive

Continuation turns are 3,223 turns with final@20 46.3%, union@20 83.3%, and union@100 93.6%. Primary-album continuations are 2,023 turns with final@20 51.7% and union@20 88.8%. The broader any-album sensitivity is 2,249 turns.

Continuation work: This is the cleanest ranker/state-use lane: preserve most-recent artist/album evidence, but learn which track to choose instead of blindly demoting same-artist candidates.

Continuation bucketTurnsShareCauseFix
Right artist IN our top-20, wrong track91653.0%within-artist RANKING — we return the artist's popular track, GT is a deep cut / next album tracksequence/album-aware discography (predict the NEXT track of the artist/album, not their hit). Artist already surfaced → cheap, high-confidence.
Heard artist NOT surfaced at all81447.0%the relevant (recent) artist crowded out by competing stale anchorskeep-strength: weight the MOST-RECENT artist up, decay older anchors so they stop crowding the pool.

Mid-conversation new-artist turns are 3,777 turns with final@20 10.0%, union@20 19.4%, and union@100 44.9%. Even explicit novelty-cue + new-artist turns are weak: n=1,990, final@20 9.0%, union@20 18.0%.

New-artist work: This is the candidate-generation lane. A trained ranker helps only when the candidate is already in union@100/200; novelty state must also change which retrievers fire and which priors dominate.

New-artist sliceTurnsFinal@20Union@20Union@100
All mid-conversation new artist377710.0%19.4%44.9%
Novelty cue + new artist19909.0%18.0%44.2%
Popularity cue + new artist12119.2%18.7%45.9%
Candidate-generation bucketTurnsShareCauseFix
Genre-fit AND popular135136.0%GT matches the requested genre and is popular (pop>=50) — a findable new artist we burygenre/era-conditioned POPULARITY prior + cross-session user CF. ~23% of ALL misses. Biggest addressable cell.
Genre-fit but obscure150039.0%matches genre, low popularity — buried among thousands of genre-mates (broad-tag dilution)stronger item encoders (the hard retrieval-capability gap). Lower ceiling.
Noise / no-fit92625.0%12% organizer DOES_NOT_MOVE + vague no-handle turnsaccept — GT contradicts the request, unwinnable.
Candidate feature catalog and role-bug examples

These are the state/ranker features and concrete role-confusion examples to preserve as implementation tests.

FeatureGrainWhy it mattersValidation
candidate_artist_roleturn x candidateSeparates seed, satisfied, contrast, history, and rejected entities instead of treating all positive mentions as anchors.Lower stale positive state rate; improve new-artist union@20 without exact/continuation regression.
artist_recency / keep_strengthturn x candidate artistContinuation needs the most recent relevant artist, while novelty needs older anchors decayed.Continuation NDCG@20 up; new-artist candidate gap not worse.
same_album_recentturn x candidate albumAlbum-mates are highly retrievable and often rank just outside top-20.Same-album continuation final@20/NDCG@20; inspect same-artist diversity side effects.
is_new_artist_for_sessionturn x candidate artistNovelty asks should favor unseen artists; continuation asks should not.New-artist union/final@20 by novelty-cue slice; continuation guardrail.
genre_or_era_conditioned_popularityturn x candidateBroad novelty asks often need a canonical/popular candidate within requested tags or era.Lift on popularity-cue and LL/J/K slices without generic popularity takeover.
user_cf_or_culture_affinityturn x candidateRaw demographics are candidate-constant, but user/culture affinity varies by track.Session-grouped CV; preferred_musical_culture slices; no user-id leakage.
constraint_satisfactionturn x candidateYear and rejection state should be modeled/guarded, not allowed to silently suppress valid targets or leak invalid ones.Zero rejection leaks; lower year-excludes-GT miss rate; no exact-turn regression.
branch_rank_bundleturn x candidateReplacing current baseline fusion needs the ranker to see which branch found the candidate and at what rank.Session-grouped CV over union@100/200 beats current baseline fusion on NDCG@20.
ExampleIntentAskCurrent anchorWhy wrongIdeal state
e0631f94 t4refinement"Dorothy is awesome... more ARTISTS with powerful female vocalists"Dorothy, Gun In My HandThe user already heard Dorothy and loved it. They’re asking for OTHER artists with the same vibe. But our system treats Dorothy as a search target (+1 anchor), so it keeps returning Dorothy-like tracks instead of bran...Dorothy should be marked “satisfied” (done, don’t search for it). The search should use the style tags (female vocalist, blues-rock) to find NEW artists with a similar sound.
b4550ccd t6refinement"Funky Child is awesome... what else can you hit me with that kind [of vibe]"11 artists: Eminem, Method Man, CZARFACE, Lupe Fiasco, Freddie Gibbs...The user only mentioned Funky Child just now, but our system drags in 11 artists from older messages (Eminem, Method Man, etc.) as active search targets. These old names flood the results, burying the fresh recommenda...Only Funky Child is relevant (and it's already heard — mark it 'satisfied'). All 11 old artists should be dropped from active search. Use classic/hip-hop style tags to find something new.
58855168 t8pivot"...keen to discover something NEW now, a DIFFERENT artist entirely" than EnyaEnyaThe user literally says they want something NEW and DIFFERENT — not Enya. But our system treats Enya as a positive search target (+1 anchor), so it finds more Enya-like music. The user is saying 'I'm done with this, s...Enya should be marked 'contrast' — meaning actively steer AWAY from Enya's sound. Use ambient/atmospheric style tags but search for genuinely different artists.
39d9709b t3pivot"...another band, completely DIFFERENT [from Tenacious D]. Heavy industrial dark"Tenacious D, Master ExploderThe user says 'completely DIFFERENT from Tenacious D' and asks for heavy industrial music. But our system uses Tenacious D (a comedy rock band) as a positive search target, pulling results toward comedy rock — the exa...Tenacious D should be marked 'contrast' — meaning steer AWAY from it. The search should be driven by the style the user asked for: industrial, heavy, dark.
f8d468c3 t8playlist_build"I like Snow Patrol... just send more alternative rock, ANYTHING"Nothing But Thieves, MISSIOThe user says 'I like Snow Patrol… just send more alternative rock, ANYTHING.' They want a wide net. But our system anchors on Nothing But Thieves and MISSIO — two bands from earlier in the conversation that the user...Drop Nothing But Thieves and MISSIO (they're history, not current). Snow Patrol is 'satisfied' (already discussed). Cast a wide net with broad alternative-rock style tags — the user literally said 'anything.'
Suggested state changes
PriorityMoveFailure classChangeValidation
P0SplitRoleless entity carryoverReplace a single positive entity interpretation with role, source_turn, recency, and anchor_strength.Extractor rerun should reduce stale-current positives; retrieval should improve new-artist union@20/100 without named-artist regression.
P0Add derived fieldSame-artist vs new-artist mode ambiguityAdd target_artist_mode: same_artist, new_artist, any_artist, or unknown, derived from cues plus history.Track target_artist_mode confusion matrix against GT artist history; route-specific union@20/100 must move.
P0Collapse downstream viewExtracted routing is not consumedCollapse routing_tags, intent_mode, and process policy into a retrieval_profile consumed by branch weights.Run routing profile A/B and report exact_probe, hidden_target_search, feature_articulation, continuation, and novelty slices.
P1Collapse and typeEra vs hard-date confusionUnify release_year_range and date hard_filters into temporal_constraint(kind, range, strength, evidence_text).No-year/soft-year ablation should recover excluded-GT turns without hurting explicit date-bound asks.
P1Keep plus normalizeKnown rejection not enforcedKeep explicit_rejections but normalize to strict IDs and alias-verified names before final filtering.Zero strict leaks; human-check name-only sample before widening the assertion.
P2Add context, not raw ranker constantsMetadata context absent from state/rankerThread available conversation_goal and user_profile fields through devset/blindset batch inputs; derive goal/culture affinity per candidate.Candidate-varying affinity must beat raw session/category/demographic features in session-grouped CV and remain usable in Blind-A inference.
Do not start hereDo not addMore raw tags / raw demographicsAvoid adding extra raw tag buckets or feeding raw demographics/category as direct candidate features.Only revisit if candidate-varying tag or affinity features beat the simpler role/routing/ranker changes.
Ideal extractor targets and minimum viable fields

Concrete state shapes to try on small API-call batches. Each target includes a cheaper minimum viable state and a fallback if the full shape is not extractable reliably.

State targetPriorityFailure classesIdeal state shapeMinimum viableExtraction probeFallbackDownstream use
role_typed_entitiesP0Roleless entity carryover; Novelty prior-anchor failure{"entities":[{"type":"artist|track|album|tag","value":"Lana Del Rey","id":"optional_catalog_id","role":"current_target|seed|satisfied|history|contrast|rejected","source_turn":4,"mentioned_current_turn":true,"anchor_strength":"0.0-1.0","use_as_retrieval_seed":true,"evidence_text":"more like Lana but not the same artist"}],"current_target_entities":["entity_id_or_value"],"history_context_entities":["entity_id_or_value"]}For each entity: type, value/id, role, source_turn, use_as_retrieval_seed.On each 10-turn pack, ask whether every prior positive artist/track is current_target, seed, satisfied, history, contrast, or rejected.Keep mentioned_entities, but derive current_turn_entity and seed_allowed booleans from current utterance plus source_turn recency.Only current_target/seed entities should fan out discography and exact-entity branches; satisfied/history entities become context or recency features.
target_artist_modeP0Same-artist vs new-artist ambiguity; New-artist union@20 gap{"target_artist_mode":"same_artist|new_artist|any_artist|unknown","confidence":"0.0-1.0","evidence_text":"different artist, same vibe","anchor_policy":"keep_recent|decay_prior|suppress_prior|suppress_rejected"}target_artist_mode enum plus evidence_text and confidence.Check whether novelty and diversify asks reliably become new_artist or any_artist instead of continuation.Derive with rules: explicit 'more by X' => same_artist; 'different/new/other artists' => new_artist; otherwise unknown.Switch retriever profile before fusion: continuation keeps album/artist branches; novelty boosts tag, CF, popularity, and similar-artist branches.
retrieval_profileP0Extracted routing is not consumed; Good tag state but union@20 gap{"retrieval_profile":"continuation|novelty|exact_probe|feature_search|hidden_target_search","branch_weights":{"artist_discography":0.2,"tag_metadata":1.4,"cf":1.2},"suppressions":["prior_artist_discography"],"fanout_topk":100}retrieval_profile enum plus positive branch hints and suppressions.For each sampled turn, label which retriever branches should get more fanout and which old anchors should be suppressed.Do not spend LLM calls first; map existing routing_tags into routing_boost/profile configs and replay.Consumes state before candidate generation, so union@20 can move instead of only final ranking.
temporal_constraintP1Era vs hard-date confusion{"temporal_constraint":{"kind":"release_date|style_era|reference_era","range":[1995,2004],"strength":"hard|soft","apply_as_filter":false,"evidence_text":"late 90s sound"}}kind, range, strength, and apply_as_filter.Ask whether the user meant literal release years or an aesthetic era; hard should be rare and explicit.Treat all inferred era ranges as soft boosts unless the user explicitly asks for a year/decade/date constraint.Hard filters only for literal constraints; style/reference eras become compatibility features.
normalized_rejectionsP1Known rejection not enforced{"rejections":[{"kind":"track|artist|album|tag|style","value":"metal","ids":["optional_catalog_or_alias_id"],"scope":"hard|soft","source_turn":8,"evidence_text":"not metal"}]}kind, value, scope, source_turn, plus strict IDs when resolver can provide them.Check whether each negative phrase is a hard rejection, a soft preference, or a contrast-only cue.Keep strict ID rejection as the canonical assertion; route name-only and style-only flags into manual/audit buckets.Finalization guardrail: strict rejected IDs must be filtered after ranking; soft rejections become negative features.
feedback_carry_forwardP0Track feedback underused; Same-album ranker failure{"track_feedback":[{"track_id":"catalog_track_id","role":"accepted|satisfied|seed|rejected|contrast","source_turn":3,"carry_forward":"seed|context|avoid|none","artist_keep_strength":"0.0-1.0","album_keep_strength":"0.0-1.0"}]}track_id, role, source_turn, carry_forward.Separate 'I liked this, keep going' from 'that satisfied the ask, now move on'.Do not rerun extraction; derive same_album_recent, same_artist_recent, and rejected_track flags from play history and existing feedback.Ranker features for same_album_recent, artist_recency, accepted-track proximity, and avoid flags.
album_artist_recency_featuresP0Same-album continuation rank loss; Good state low recall{"candidate_features":{"same_album_recent":true,"same_artist_recent":true,"artist_last_seen_turn_delta":2,"album_last_seen_turn_delta":1,"accepted_anchor_similarity":"0.0-1.0"}}No new extractor field required; compute same_album_recent, same_artist_recent, and recency deltas from history.No LLM probe. Verify feature computation on same-album and clean-hit packs.Use binary same_album_recent and same_artist_recent only.Trained ranker/reranker feature; should improve final@20 without changing union@20.
positive_tag_compatibilityP1Positive tag retrieval gap{"positive_tags":[{"raw":"late-night","canonical":"late night","role":"target_feature|context|contrast","confidence":"0.0-1.0","source_turn":2}],"negative_tags":[{"raw":"too heavy","canonical":"heavy metal","scope":"soft"}]}canonical tag, role, confidence, source_turn.Check whether tags are current target features or merely old context; normalize synonyms without adding many new fields.Keep raw positive_tags; add candidate tag-overlap and tag-compatibility features from catalog metadata.Candidate generation/ranker compatibility feature; not a standalone reason to rerun the whole extractor.
exact_reference_guardP1Exact entity success control; Named artist ranker failure{"exact_references":[{"kind":"track|artist|album","value":"Dreams","ids":["catalog_id"],"confidence":"0.0-1.0","protect_topk":20,"evidence_text":"play Dreams"}]}kind, value/id, confidence, protect_topk.Verify explicit named-track controls stay exact and named-artist cases protect artist candidates without overfitting common words.Use strict title/artist lexical resolver and branch protection; avoid broad fuzzy matching for short/common titles.Candidate protection and ranker exactness feature; also regression guardrail for any state rewrite.
goal_profile_contextP2Metadata context absent from state/ranker{"goal_context":{"listener_goal":"discover energetic songs for workouts","category":"J","specificity":"LH","preferred_musical_culture":"US mainstream","use_for":"routing|candidate_affinity|analysis_only","candidate_varying_features":["culture_conditioned_popularity","goal_text_match","user_cf_affinity"]}}Thread existing listener_goal, category, specificity, and preferred_musical_culture from raw rows into the state/ranker pack.No expensive per-turn extraction first; Blind-A raw rows already have these fields. Test whether metadata improves candidate-varying affinity features in CV.Use metadata only for slicing and diagnostics until candidate-varying features show lift; do not emulate fields that Blind-A already provides.Novelty and popularity retrieval/ranker features; raw session constants are not enough.
State confusion / field economy plan

More fields can make the extractor and retrieval logic less clear. This table names overlapping state surfaces and the simplification rule to test before spending on broader extraction.

Confusing surfaceRiskDecisionField-economy moveSmall test
mentioned_entities treated as positive anchorsThe extractor stores prior liked/satisfied/history mentions in a field that downstream retrieval reads as current intent.Split roles instead of adding more positive-entity fields.Replace positive/current ambiguity with role_typed_entities and seed_allowed/use_as_retrieval_seed.On stale and novelty packs, every old artist/track should be satisfied, history, contrast, or rejected unless the current user explicitly reuses it.
turn_intent, intent_mode, routing_tags, and process_constraints all describe modeMultiple mode fields can disagree, and the current config does not consume routing_boost anyway.Collapse downstream to one retrieval_profile plus target_artist_mode.Keep raw fields for QA/debug; expose only retrieval_profile to candidate generation.For novelty packs, retrieval_profile should suppress old-artist fanout and boost tag/CF/popularity branches.
release_year_range plus date hard_filtersThe system can misread a style-era cue as a literal release-date filter.Collapse to temporal_constraint(kind, range, strength, apply_as_filter).One temporal object beats separate range/filter fields.In year-excludes-GT samples, style_era/reference_era should be soft and apply_as_filter=false unless the user asks for literal years.
positive_tags, lyrical_theme, attributes, and genre descriptorsMore tag-like fields can fragment the same signal and dilute retrieval/ranker features.Keep specialized fields, but canonicalize and assign roles instead of adding more raw buckets.Use positive_tag_compatibility with raw/canonical/role/source_turn; keep lyrical_theme for lyric/theme routing.Positive-tag gap samples should preserve current target tags and avoid promoting old context tags.
explicit_rejections mixed with soft dislikes or contrast cuesA contrast cue can become an over-hard filter, while true rejected IDs can still leak without normalization.Keep rejections separate from contrast; normalize strict IDs and aliases.Use normalized_rejections with hard/soft scope and keep contrast as an entity role.Rejected-ID samples should have zero strict leaks; name/style-only samples stay audit-only until hand-labeled.
track_feedback, referenced_track_ids, and exact referencesA referenced track can mean exact target, satisfied prior item, seed, contrast, or rejection.Keep exact_reference_guard separate from feedback_carry_forward.Exact references protect candidates; feedback roles decide seed/context/avoid/none.Exact controls stay final@20; same-album samples compute recency features without making every played track a seed.
candidate features requested from the extractorAsking the LLM for derived ranker features increases cost and inconsistency.Do not extract deterministic features.Compute same_album_recent, same_artist_recent, artist_recency, album_recency, branch ranks, and exactness outside the LLM.Same-album/ranker packs should test feature computation and trained scoring, not state reruns.
organizer session metadata blended into turn stateSession constants can look important but cannot rank candidates within a turn unless transformed.Keep organizer metadata in a separate goal_context namespace.Use listener_goal/category/specificity/profile as context, slices, or candidate-varying affinity; do not ask the extractor to recreate them.Goal/profile experiments must beat raw-constant baselines in session-grouped CV.
Organizer metadata decision

Use organizer session metadata directly when available. Emulate or extract it only if a target split lacks it and a measured candidate-varying feature has shown value.

Verified Blind-A raw rows include conversation_goal, user_profile, and goal_progress_assessments on 80/80 rows. Current blindset inference does not pass those objects into retrieval/compiler state.

ItemBlind-A statusEvidenceReport decision
Blind-A raw HF rowsavailable80 rows in talkpl-ai/TalkPlayData-Challenge-Blind-A test; columns include session_id, user_id, session_date, user_profile, conversation_goal, conversations, goal_progress_assessments.Organizer metadata is available in Blind-A raw rows; do not pay extractor calls to emulate it first.
conversation_goalavailablemissing=0, empty=0; keys=category, listener_goal, specificity.Thread category, specificity, and listener_goal into retrieval/ranker context when testing metadata features.
user_profileavailablemissing=0, empty=0; keys=age, age_group, country_code, country_name, gender, preferred_language, preferred_musical_culture, user_id, user_split.Use preferred_musical_culture/profile as context or candidate-varying affinity; do not infer demographics from text.
goal_progress_assessmentspartially labeledmissing=0, empty=0; keys=goal_progress_assessment, turn_number; non-null assessments=210/290; last-row assessment non-null=60/80.Use with leakage review. Visible progress can be useful, but do not treat it as a hidden GT label.
current blindset inference pathmetadata dropped before retrievalrun_inference_blindset.py builds batch_data with user_query, user_id, and session_memory only; output metadata keeps session_id, user_id, turn_number.The next implementation step is plumbing available metadata into the compiler/ranker input, not emulating Blind-A metadata.
SourceUse directly?Emulate / extract?Ranking shapeFirst test
conversation_goal.listener_goalYes. Feed as immutable session context for routing/query text and as a goal-text compatibility feature.Do not emulate for Blind-A: it is available. Only extract a replacement for a future split that lacks listener_goal.Candidate-varying goal_text_match, goal-conditioned popularity, or retrieval profile; not a raw constant score.First plumb listener_goal through run_inference_blindset/devset batch inputs; then compare novelty/broad-goal packs with and without it.
conversation_goal.category A-KUse for slicing, guardrails, and coarse route priors when available.Do not spend extractor calls predicting A-K for Blind-A. If useful, derive operational route labels from listener_goal/state instead of matching organizer codes.Category-conditioned retriever profile or evaluation slice; raw category is candidate-constant within a turn.Measure union@20/final@20 by A-K and only promote categories that map to different successful retriever profiles.
conversation_goal.specificity LL/LH/HL/HHUse as guardrail slices: HH exactness should not regress; LL broad discovery needs better candidate generation.Do not emulate the code itself for Blind-A. If needed, extract simpler operational fields: exactness_required, broad_discovery, constraint_strength.Route exact/high-specificity turns toward entity protection; route low-specificity turns toward popularity/CF/tag exploration.Run exact-reference controls by HH-like turns and novelty-popularity packs by LL-like turns.
user_profile.preferred_musical_cultureUse as context for culture-conditioned popularity, CF priors, and response style.Do not infer culture from conversation when profile is present; that is noisy and can create bias/leakage.Candidate-varying culture/user affinity, not a constant feature attached to every candidate.Test culture-conditioned popularity or user-CF affinity on new-artist and popularity-cue packs.
age_group, gender, country, preferred_languageUse mainly for response style, analysis slices, leakage/fairness checks, and maybe language/local catalog priors.Do not extract or infer these from conversation text.Only candidate-varying features after validation; raw demographics showed no within-turn ranking lift as constants.Keep as guardrail slices while testing stronger behavioral features first.
goal_progress_assessmentsUse only after leakage review. Prior visible progress may help; the current row's assessment should be treated carefully.Do not emulate first. If used, prefer visible previous-turn labels and audit current-turn labels for leakage.Satisfaction/move-on signal, not a hidden GT label.Ablate previous-turn assessment features separately from conversation text/state features and inspect exact-target cases.
Holistic work plan
AreaCostDecisionFirst test
State extraction rerunexpensiveRun only for role/recency and target_artist_mode changes.Use P0_roleless_stale_entity_failure, P0_novelty_prior_anchor_failure, and P0_new_artist_union20_gap_failure before a full devset rerun.
State field economycheap before any extractor rerunCollapse or derive confusing overlapping fields before adding new schema surface.Use the state confusion plan and replay packs to confirm fewer fields give clearer extraction and no recall regression.
Organizer session metadatacheap when fields are availableUse listener_goal and preferred_musical_culture as context/affinity experiments; do not pay LLM calls to emulate organizer fields first.Run goal/culture candidate-varying features on novelty/popularity packs; keep category/specificity as slices and guardrails.
Routing / retrieval profilescheap to mediumDo before adding new retrievers: wire extracted routing tags into branch weights/profiles.Replay routing profiles on P0_new_artist_union20_gap_failure and report union@20/100.
Trained state-aware rankermediumHigh priority, but it must consume state/candidate features instead of raw session constants.Use P0_good_state_ranker_near_miss_failure, P0_named_artist_ranker_failure, and P0_same_album_ranker_failure, then session-grouped CV over union@100/200.
Album and artist recencymediumBuild candidate-level features; no extraction rerun required if history is already available.Add same_album_recent and artist_recency to the ranker and guardrail new-artist slices.
Temporal and rejection guardrailscheapTreat as deterministic replay tests, not a broad state rewrite.Use P1_temporal_constraint_failure and P1_rejection_guardrail_failure; assert zero strict rejection leaks and compare no-year/soft-year replay.
New novelty retrieversmedium to highOnly after routing/profile tests show union@20 remains weak.Try genre/era-conditioned popularity, user-CF/culture affinity, and tag-first retrieval on novelty samples.
Raw tags / raw demographicslow but distractingDo not make this the next big extraction project.Only revisit as candidate-varying tag compatibility or culture/user affinity features.
Small subset experiment packs
PackClass typeTurnsTargetHypothesisState targetsState terms to checkSuccess metric
P0_roleless_stale_entity_failurefailure1010A role-typed state rerun should stop stale/satisfied entities from acting like current anchors.role_typed_entities; feedback_carry_forwardFor each entity: type, value/id, role, source_turn, use_as_retrieval_seed. | track_id, role, source_turn, carry_forward.State QA first: stale-current-positive count drops on all sampled turns. Retrieval QA second: union@20/100 should not get worse, and target branch rank should improve on at least some samples.
P0_novelty_prior_anchor_failurefailure1010Novelty/diversify turns should not keep old artists as active positive anchors.role_typed_entities; target_artist_mode; retrieval_profileFor each entity: type, value/id, role, source_turn, use_as_retrieval_seed. | target_artist_mode enum plus evidence_text and confidence. | retrieval_profile enum plus positive branch hints and suppressions.State QA: prior anchors are not current positive seeds. Recall QA: best_branch_rank or union@20 improves for novelty/new-artist targets.
P0_new_artist_union20_gap_failurefailure1010Novelty/new-artist turns need a different retrieval profile, not just better final ranking.target_artist_mode; retrieval_profile; goal_profile_contexttarget_artist_mode enum plus evidence_text and confidence. | retrieval_profile enum plus positive branch hints and suppressions. | Thread existing listener_goal, category, specificity, and preferred_musical_culture from raw rows into the state/ranker pack.Primary: GT enters union@20 or improves best_branch_rank. Secondary: final@20 after a stable ranker should improve without continuation regression.
P1_temporal_constraint_failurefailure1010Temporal state should distinguish style-era cues from hard release-date filters.temporal_constraintkind, range, strength, and apply_as_filter.Target remains eligible and best_branch_rank improves under soft/no-year ablation; explicit date-bound turns stay clean.
P1_rejection_guardrail_failurefailure1010Rejected entities should never leak into final top-20.normalized_rejectionskind, value, scope, source_turn, plus strict IDs when resolver can provide them.Zero strict rejected ID leakage; name-only flags are hand-labeled before widening assertions.
P0_named_artist_ranker_failurefailure1010Named-artist turns usually have the right entity but still lose the right track in ranking/finalization.exact_reference_guard; role_typed_entitieskind, value/id, confidence, protect_topk. | For each entity: type, value/id, role, source_turn, use_as_retrieval_seed.GT moves into final top-20 without hurting exact-track or rejection guardrails.
P0_same_album_ranker_failurefailure1010Album-continuation turns need album/artist recency features, not another state extraction pass.feedback_carry_forward; album_artist_recency_featurestrack_id, role, source_turn, carry_forward. | No new extractor field required; compute same_album_recent, same_artist_recent, and recency deltas from history.Same-album continuation final@20/NDCG@20 improves while new-artist slices do not regress.
P1_positive_tag_retrieval_gap_failurefailure1010Good tag state can still fail candidate generation when stale anchors or weak routing dominate.positive_tag_compatibility; retrieval_profilecanonical tag, role, confidence, source_turn. | retrieval_profile enum plus positive branch hints and suppressions.GT enters union@20 or best_branch_rank improves; tag-overlap final@20 does not regress.
P0_good_state_ranker_near_miss_failurefailure1010Some extraction is already good enough; these turns should test state-aware ranking rather than an extractor rerun.feedback_carry_forward; album_artist_recency_features; exact_reference_guardtrack_id, role, source_turn, carry_forward. | No new extractor field required; compute same_album_recent, same_artist_recent, and recency deltas from history. | kind, value/id, confidence, protect_topk.GT moves into final top-20 while rejection/year/exact guardrails stay clean.
POS_exact_entity_success_controlpositive_control1010Exact or very explicit entity cases should remain solved after any state/ranker change.exact_reference_guardkind, value/id, confidence, protect_topk.All sampled exact-entity controls remain final@20 after state/routing/ranker changes.
POS_clean_final_hit_controlpositive_control1010Clean final hits should stay stable while failure classes improve.role_typed_entities; album_artist_recency_features; positive_tag_compatibilityFor each entity: type, value/id, role, source_turn, use_as_retrieval_seed. | No new extractor field required; compute same_album_recent, same_artist_recent, and recency deltas from history. | canonical tag, role, confidence, source_turn.All sampled clean-hit controls remain final@20; ranks should not degrade materially.
Sample turns to replay

Use the JSON file for the full payload, including current user text, baseline ranks, diagnostics, expected change, and state snapshot.

Sample idPackClass typeGTBaseline ranksExpected change
0b9d547f-e748-464a-90e2-2199149f915c::t6P0_roleless_stale_entity_failurefailureGive It To Me Baby by Rick Jamesfinal=158; fused=218; branch=101After extraction rerun, stale prior entities should move out of current positive anchors or get lower anchor_strength.
e66c6a88-88ba-4117-9114-363bfa96294a::t7P0_roleless_stale_entity_failurefailureTest Drive by John Powellfinal=117; fused=178; branch=101After extraction rerun, stale prior entities should move out of current positive anchors or get lower anchor_strength.
0858f444-c9af-4f08-a9fc-2a731a24182b::t5P0_roleless_stale_entity_failurefailureArmature by Emptysetfinal=368; fused=384; branch=101After extraction rerun, stale prior entities should move out of current positive anchors or get lower anchor_strength.

Counting reconciliation and caveats

These notes keep definition-sensitive counts honest, especially album continuation and rejection matching.

ClaimReport valueAlternate valueDecision
GT primary album already heard2,0232,249 any-albumUse primary-album as the canonical strict album-continuation count; keep any-album as broader sensitivity.
Album miss fused-rank bucketsprimary misses=978; buckets sum=978album counterfactual miss_total=964; 21-100=479; 101-1000=326; absent=159Use the trace proxy for failure anatomy and the counterfactual for measured upside. Do not compare raw bucket values without the denominator.
Rejection leak turns15997 strict ID; 62 additional name-onlyTreat broad count as an upper audit bound and strict count as the verified lower bound until a hand sample labels name-only cases.
Routing tag countsexact_entity_probe=1,605; hidden_target_search=391Sample extrapolations can differ.Keep the full-trace counts. The important bug is not extraction frequency; routing_boost is empty, so tags are not consumed by weighted routing.
Current user names GT artist1,671Alias/partial-name methods can move the count by roughly tens of turns.Rates are the stable takeaway: named-artist turns have very high union@20 but lower final@20, so they are a track-selection/ranking slice.
Stale positive entity turns3,807More inclusive substring heuristics count slightly more.Use as a directional state QA metric, not a perfect semantic label.
Exact named-track turns92Looser title-substring methods count more.Keep the stricter count to avoid false positives from common words.

Gap Map: Candidate Pool vs Final Ranking

This section uses the current run's fusion/ranking trace to classify failures. Treat the current fusion method as baseline instrumentation; the target system is state-aware retrieval profiles plus a trained or calibrated candidate scorer.

Bucketed by union@20/100

How to read it

Items in union20_* are already available to a final candidate scorer. Items in union100_near_miss are close enough that query cleanup, retrieval-profile routing, or a trained ranker may recover them. Items beyond union@100 need stronger retrieval or better state-targeting.

Mechanism buckets

Post-fusion demotion clues

The same-artist pattern points to ANCHOR_ARTIST_DEMOTE_BY_POLICY under diversify_artists.

Per-branch recall when branch fires

Best branch for current focus bucket

Dimension

Detail table

Trained ranker recipe

Candidate pool: start with union@200 for training and compare union@100 / union@1000 serving costs. Label: single GT track per turn. Model: logistic scorer or LambdaMART before cross-encoder complexity. Features: min branch rank, per-branch ranks, number of branches hitting top-20/top-100, trusted branch hit, resolved artist/track match, same-artist-as-accepted/rejected, positive and negative tag overlap, release-year in range or distance, popularity bucket, turn number, intent mode, exploration policy, and routing tags.

The report's ceiling ladder is the reason to try this: a good scorer over union@100 has a 66.2% top-20 ceiling before any new retriever work.

Evidence Notes

Sources: /Users/npatta01/data/projects/music-conversational-music-recomender-2026/exp/inference/devset/v0plus_compiler_all_retrievers_devset_trace.jsonl, /Users/npatta01/data/projects/music-conversational-music-recomender-2026/exp/inference/devset/v0plus_compiler_all_retrievers_devset.json, /Users/npatta01/data/projects/music-conversational-music-recomender-2026/evaluator/exp/ground_truth/devset.json, /Users/npatta01/data/projects/music-conversational-music-recomender-2026/configs/v0plus_compiler_all_retrievers_devset.yaml. The report streams the trace and uses LanceDB catalog metadata only for lightweight GT labels, artist matching, tags, release years, and popularity buckets.
